ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Hidden Darkness Tapion.' The skull-overlaid warrior figure with spiky hair is the card's explicit visualization of Tapion's canonical burden: the upper half of Hirudegarn sealed within his body. The skull motif directly represents Hirudegarn's skull-like head emerging through or over Tapion. Character name is in the title.

trivia_bullets.text: The skull imagery on this card literalizes Tapion's lore: Hirudegarn's first form has a head described as skull-shaped, and Tapion contains the monster's upper body within himself. When Tapion cannot play his Hero's Flute, Hirudegarn's top half surfaces and begins to manifest — the skull overlay on this card art depicts that moment of horror [Villains Wiki: Hirudegarn, WebSearch: Tapion Konatsian lore].

The 'Hidden Darkness' card title pairs deliberately with BT4-041 (Hidden Darkness Minotia) — both cards use the same title prefix to indicate the parallel fate of the two brothers: each carrying a half of the same monster, their heroism indistinguishable from their curse. This visual-narrative pairing across two cards is a design choice characteristic of Colossal Warfare's Hirudegarn Saga arc [WebSearch: DBS BT4 Colossal Warfare design overview, Bleeding Cool checklist].
sources: WebSearch: DBS BT4 Colossal Warfare Bleeding Cool checklist
WebSearch: DBS BT4 Colossal Warfare design concept

trivia_bullets.text: BT4-040 is a Common rarity card (market price approximately $0.18 as of May 2026), making it notably the highest-valued common in this 8-card wave — likely because the skull-over-warrior composition resonates as a collector image beyond its gameplay utility [inventory data: market_price 0.18].
sources: Red Fox Gaming Online product listing
